renderedFields.description:
As a QA Engineer,
I want to validate negative scenarios in the Elements module,
so that invalid inputs are handled correctly and the UI remains stable under edge conditions.

Target Application:
https://demoqa.com/elements

✅ Acceptance Criteria

AC1 – Email Validation

  • Invalid email input triggers validation error on #userEmail
  • Output section (#output) is not displayed for invalid input

AC2 – Web Tables Validation

  • Non-numeric values in Age/Salary fields block submission
  • Registration modal remains open on validation failure

AC3 – Radio Button Validation

  • “No” option (#noRadio) remains disabled
  • Clicking the option does not trigger any state change

AC4 – UI Stability

  • UI remains stable under obstruction (e.g., overlays)
  • Elements remain interactable via scroll/visibility handling

📸 UI Validation Reference

Annotated screenshots have been attached to illustrate expected UI behavior for each validation scenario.

The screenshots cover:

  • Text Box Validation
    • Invalid email → validation error, no output displayed
    • Valid input → output section rendered
  • Web Tables Validation
    • Invalid numeric input → submission blocked, modal remains open
  • Radio Button Behavior
    • Disabled option → no interaction or state change

These screenshots serve as visual references for expected behavior and should be used during test execution to verify UI responses.

🧪 Test Data

  • Invalid email formats (e.g., test@domain, missing TLD)
  • Non-numeric values for numeric fields (e.g., abc, 12ab)
  • Empty/null inputs

🎯 Notes

  • Screenshots are provided as reference (expected behavior)
  • During execution, actual results should be validated against Acceptance Criteria
  • Any deviation should be logged as a defect with supporting evidence
